nodes.context: Major drug trafficking cartel led by Pablo Escobar, active from 1976 to 1993.
Drug trafficking cartel based in Cali, founded by the Rodríguez Orejuela brothers, active 1977-1995.
Drug cartel operating in northern Valle del Cauca, prominent after Cali and Medellín fragmented, active 1990-2012.
Drug trafficking cartel active from 1999 to 2004.
Colombian criminal bands that emerged after the fall of major cartels.

nodes.context: Paramilitary group and drug trafficking organization; leader Pedro Guerrero died in 2010.
Successor paramilitary group and drug trafficking organization.

Former guerrilla movement, considered a drug cartel as of 2013.
Former enforcement wing for the Medellín Cartel; inherited turf and business connections.
Leftist guerrilla group that controlled coca-growing regions and taxed coca-paste sales.
Right-wing paramilitary group that grew out of private armies of cocaine cartels; demobilized in 2006.
Mexico-based drug cartel that held major influence over cocaine trafficking from Colombia.
